legacyMigration.runningHint: This can take a moment for large libraries. Please don't close this tab.
legacyMigration.deleteStorageConfirmDescription: This permanently deletes the old IndexedDB database (video-editor-db) from this browser. Your workspace folder is unaffected. Only do this after you've verified the migration succeeded.
legacyMigration.promptDescription: Existing projects from before the workspace folder migration are in this browser's IndexedDB. Bring them into your workspace so you can see them alongside new projects.

legacyMigrationErrors.retryStillFailing_one: Retry completed with {{count}} item still failing.
legacyMigrationErrors.retryStillFailing_other: Retry completed with {{count}} items still failing.
legacyMigrationErrors.retryHint: Retry to copy anything still missing from the legacy database.

workspaceGate.unsupportedBrowserDescription: FreeCut requires a Chromium-based browser (Chrome, Edge, Brave, Arc) for its workspace folder storage. Firefox and Safari don't yet support the File System Access API.
workspaceGate.pickDescription: FreeCut stores all your projects, media, and caches as plain files in a folder you control. Pick any folder on disk — your projects will live there.
workspaceGate.pickTip: Tip: pick a folder in Dropbox, iCloud, or Google Drive to sync projects across devices. You can move it later.
workspaceGate.reconnectDescription: We need permission to access {{name}} again. This happens automatically between browser sessions.
workspaceGate.folderPermissionDenied: Permission was not granted. Choose a folder and allow read/write access to continue.
workspaceGate.folderPickFailed: FreeCut could not open that folder. Try a normal folder you can edit, then grant read/write access.
workspaceGate.reconnectPermissionDenied: Permission was denied. Reconnect the workspace or choose a different folder.

export.title: Export on the Web
export.description: Render your videos locally with your browser.

free.question: Is FreeCut really free?
free.answer: Yes, FreeCut is completely free and open source under the MIT license. There are no hidden fees, subscriptions, or watermarks.

install.question: Do I need to install anything?
install.answer: No installation required. FreeCut runs entirely in your browser. Just open the website and start editing.

storage.question: Where are my videos stored?
storage.answer: Your videos and projects are stored locally in your browser or referenced to your local files using modern storage APIs.

browsers.question: What browsers are supported?
browsers.answerP1: FreeCut currently works best in Chrome or Edge 113+. It relies on modern browser APIs like WebGPU, WebCodecs, OPFS, and File System Access, so the full workflow is currently Chromium-first.
browsers.answerP2: Brave users: The File System Access API is disabled by default. To enable it, navigate to brave://flags/#file-system-access-api, set it to Enabled, and relaunch the browser.

exportFormats.question: What export formats are supported?
exportFormats.answer: Video: MP4, MOV, WebM, MKV. Audio: MP3, AAC, WAV (PCM). The current export UI exposes H.264, H.265, VP8, VP9, and AV1 with low, medium, high, and ultra quality presets.

future.question: Future Improvements
future.answer: The current focus is beta hardening: align product docs with shipped behavior, tighten accessibility and test coverage, and make defaults like FPS, snap, preview quality, and export settings flow through the real editor.

shoutout.question: Special shoutout
shoutout.answerP1: A huge thank you to <link>Mediabunny</link> for making browser-based video encoding easy. This project wouldn't exist without their amazing work!
